success_surface: Cell-Level Formula Manipulation with Error Handling
effective_behaviors: Explore spreadsheet structure (sheet names, column headers, dimensions) before implementing formulas
Wrap lookup formulas (INDEX/MATCH, VLOOKUP) with IFERROR or equivalent error handling when instructed to leave cells blank on mismatch
Use absolute column references ($C:$C, $D:$D) with relative row references for copy-down formulas
Generate formulas dynamically using loops and string formatting instead of hardcoding individual cell formulas
Verify formula cells after writing by reading back their values and comparing against expected pattern
Preserve workbook integrity by saving to output path rather than modifying source file in-place
Apply openpyxl library for formula-based cell manipulation to maintain Excel-native formula behavior
evidence: Agent first queried sheet names, column headers, and data layout before creating formulas
Column references were made absolute ($D:$D, $C:$C) while row indices remained relative (A2, A3, ...)
Loop generated 18 unique formulas programmatically using f-string formatting with row_idx variable
Agent printed first 5 formula cells to verify correct pattern after saving
Output saved to '/mnt/data/outputs/...' path distinct from source file path
Openpyxl.load_workbook() and ws.cell().value assignments used for direct formula manipulation

memory_items.title: Pre-Modification Structure Verification
memory_items.lesson: Always inspect spreadsheet structure (sheets, headers, dimensions) before implementing changes to ensure correct targeting
memory_items.generalization: Query wb.sheetnames, ws.max_row, ws.max_column, and sample header values before any write operation to validate scope assumptions.

memory_items.title: Error-Handling in Lookup Formulas
memory_items.lesson: Wrap lookup functions with IFERROR or similar error handlers when the task requires graceful failure handling
memory_items.generalization: Convert lookup formulas like MATCH/VLOOKUP/INDEX-MATCH to IFERROR(formula, blank_or_default_value) per instruction requirements.

memory_items.title: Relative and Absolute Reference Strategy
memory_items.lesson: When copying formulas across rows, use absolute column references for fixed ranges and relative references for dynamic lookups
memory_items.generalization: In copy-down scenarios, anchor lookup/sort ranges with $ signs (e.g., $C:$C) while leaving row indices unanchored (e.g., A2, A3).

memory_items.title: Batch Formula Generation Pattern
memory_items.lesson: Generate multiple cell formulas dynamically using loops and string interpolation instead of individual assignments
memory_items.generalization: Use range iteration with f-string formatting to create row-specific formulas programmatically for multi-cell modifications.

memory_items.title: Post-Write Formula Verification
memory_items.lesson: Always read back and display a sample of modified formulas to confirm they were written correctly before considering task complete
memory_items.generalization: After applying formulas to a range, print 3-5 representative cells to verify formula strings match expected pattern.

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Lessons

### Formula Construction Best Practices
- **Structure Verification First**: Before writing formulas, always query worksheet metadata (sheet names, max row/column, header values) to confirm target scope matches instructions.
- **Lookup Formula Error Handling**: Wrap lookup functions (INDEX/MATCH, VLOOKUP, XLOOKUP) with IFERROR or equivalent error-handling wrapper when the task specifies handling unmatched values gracefully.
- **Reference Anchoring Strategy**: For formulas intended to be copied across rows, use absolute column references ($C:$C, $D:$D) for search/result ranges while keeping row references relative (A2, A3...) to enable proper copy-down behavior.
- **Dynamic Batch Generation**: Generate formulas for multi-cell ranges using loop iteration and string formatting rather than manual per-cell assignment; this reduces errors and improves maintainability.
- **Post-Writing Validation**: After applying formulas to a range, immediately read and log sample cells to verify the formula strings match the expected pattern before finalizing the task.
- **Library Choice Confirmation**: Use openpyxl for formula insertion tasks to preserve native Excel formula syntax; pandas cannot maintain live formula references in the same way.
